title: Signal Routing -- current lug routing rules and the replacement for deprecated SIGNAL routing
what: Signal routing (routed_to=SIGNAL) is fully deprecated. No new lug should ever use routed_to=SIGNAL. The canonical routing values are: LOCAL (stays in this spoke), FRAMEWORK (improvement relevant to all spokes, delivered as an impl/task lug via the hub-spoke transport), SPOKE/{spoke_id} (work for a specific named spoke), and ASSESSOR (model telemetry deposited to hub assessor inbox). User-action-required alerts use type=signal lugs written directly to {hub_path}/WAI-Hub/signals/inbox/ with no routed_to field — these are messages to the human, not directives to agents. The old pattern of creating a signal lug with routed_to=SIGNAL as a fleet-wide patch mechanism is retired. Framework fixes and improvements now flow as impl/task lugs with routed_to=FRAMEWORK: the framework implements the fix, publishes a teaching, and spokes absorb it at next session start. The decision table is: if a human must read it and decide — write a type=signal to hub signals/inbox/; if an agent can implement it without human input — create a task/impl lug; if it improves the framework for all spokes — task/impl with routed_to=FRAMEWORK; if it belongs to another specific spoke — routed_to=SPOKE/{id}. Existing signal lugs encountered with routed_to=SIGNAL must be converted to impl/task lugs with routed_to=LOCAL or routed_to=FRAMEWORK — they must not be delivered to any hub inbox and must not be skipped. The scope_verified_by field is required on all lugs with routed_to != LOCAL, recording who confirmed the routing decision.
why: The SIGNAL routing mechanism was deprecated because it conflated two distinct concerns: fleet-wide urgent patches (now handled by FRAMEWORK impl lugs plus teachings) and user-action-required alerts (now type=signal to hub signals/inbox/). The old mechanism created ambiguity about who acts on what and caused agents to misroute spoke-specific issues as fleet-wide urgencies.

use_cases.title: Routing a framework improvement
use_cases.trigger: Agent identifies a gap in the WAI lug schema that affects all spokes
use_cases.outcome: Agent creates a task lug with routed_to=FRAMEWORK, delivers it to framework/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/, framework implements and publishes a teaching

use_cases.title: Alerting the user to a decision they must make
use_cases.trigger: Agent cannot resolve a breaking conflict without human input
use_cases.outcome: Agent writes a type=signal lug to {hub_path}/WAI-Hub/signals/inbox/ — no routed_to field needed; user sees it at next session start

use_cases.title: Converting a deprecated SIGNAL lug
use_cases.trigger: Agent encounters an open lug with routed_to=SIGNAL
use_cases.outcome: Agent rewrites routed_to to LOCAL or FRAMEWORK based on scope, stores as impl/task lug — does not deliver to hub inbox

patterns.name: SIGNAL routing is banned
patterns.description: routed_to=SIGNAL must never appear in a new lug. If found in an existing lug, convert it to LOCAL or FRAMEWORK. There are no valid use cases remaining.

patterns.name: Human alerts vs agent directives
patterns.description: type=signal to hub signals/inbox/ is for humans only — agents show it but cannot resolve it. task/impl lugs with appropriate routed_to values are for agents.

how.trigger: Any lug creation or delivery decision requiring routing classification
steps_summary: 1. Determine who acts: human-only decision → type=signal to hub signals/inbox/; agent-executable → task/impl lug
2. Determine scope: only this spoke → LOCAL; all spokes (framework) → FRAMEWORK; specific spoke → SPOKE/{id}; telemetry → ASSESSOR
3. Set routed_to to one of: LOCAL, FRAMEWORK, SPOKE/{spoke_id}, ASSESSOR
4. Set scope_verified_by: user, ozi, framework, or auto-signal
5. For FRAMEWORK: deliver impl/task lug to framework/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/ immediately
6. For SPOKE/{id}: look up path in hub-registry.json, deliver to {path}/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/
constraints: routed_to=SIGNAL is fully deprecated — zero valid use cases
type=signal lugs have no routed_to field — they are written directly to hub signals/inbox/
scope_verified_by is required on all lugs with routed_to != LOCAL

triggers: Every lug creation (routing decision required at creation time)
Encountering an existing lug with routed_to=SIGNAL (conversion required)
not_when: There are no conditions under which routed_to=SIGNAL is valid

inputs: Lug scope and audience analysis (who implements, who reads)
outputs: Lug with routed_to set to LOCAL, FRAMEWORK, SPOKE/{id}, or ASSESSOR
state_changes: FRAMEWORK lugs: delivered to framework incoming/, completed locally after delivery
SPOKE/{id} lugs: delivered to target spoke incoming/, outgoing/ audit copy created

constraints: routed_to=SIGNAL is banned — converting found instances is mandatory, not optional
type=signal to hub signals/inbox/ requires: title, body, source_spoke, created_at, status=open fields
body: Signal routing (routed_to=SIGNAL) is fully and permanently deprecated. No new lug should ever use routed_to=SIGNAL — there are zero valid conditions for its use. The canonical routing values for all lugs are: LOCAL (work stays in this spoke), FRAMEWORK (improvement affecting all spokes — delivered as impl/task lug via hub-spoke transport), SPOKE/{spoke_id} (work for a specific named spoke identified in hub-registry.json), and ASSESSOR (model telemetry deposited to hub assessor inbox). The mechanism that replaced SIGNAL routing operates in two distinct channels based on audience. For agent-executable work: create impl/task lugs with routed_to=FRAMEWORK or routed_to=SPOKE/{id}. For user-action-required alerts: create type=signal lugs written directly to {hub_path}/WAI-Hub/signals/inbox/{id}.json — these require no routed_to field and are shown to the user at session start but are not actionable by agents. If an agent encounters an existing lug with routed_to=SIGNAL it must convert the lug to LOCAL or FRAMEWORK based on scope analysis before doing anything else — conversion is mandatory, not optional. Converting means rewriting the routed_to field and storing the lug in the correct bytype/ path; it does not mean delivering it to hub inbox. The scope decision rule is: human-only decision (no agent can resolve without human input) → type=signal to hub/signals/inbox/; agent-resolvable work → task/impl lug with LOCAL/FRAMEWORK/SPOKE routing; informational with no action required → learning lug or memory file. Framework publishing a teaching is the correct mechanism for fleet-wide behavioral updates — not signals.
done_when: No new lug created in any session contains routed_to=SIGNAL — Ozi routing gate rejects any lug with this value at dispatch time
An existing lug with routed_to=SIGNAL is converted to routed_to=LOCAL or routed_to=FRAMEWORK before the session ends, and the conversion is verified by checking the file on disk
A user-action-required alert is written to {hub_path}/WAI-Hub/signals/inbox/ as a type=signal lug with title, body, source_spoke, created_at, and status=open fields — no routed_to field present
A FRAMEWORK-scoped impl lug is delivered to framework/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/ at creation time, not queued for closeout delivery
scope_verified_by is set on all lugs with routed_to != LOCAL, recording one of: user, ozi, framework, auto-signal
